items.back: When CPDLC is available and aircraft is outside VHF coverage, CPDLC must be used as the primary means of communication. Voice backup (HF, SATCOM, Third Party) must exist. Per 14-3-1(a, c).

items.front: Oceanic CPDLC: when may voice be used for connected aircraft?
items.back: When it provides operational advantage and/or when workload or equipment capabilities demand. Per 14-3-1(b).

items.front: Oceanic CPDLC: response to pilot-initiated CPDLC
items.back: Controller response should also be via CPDLC. Use the standard message set rather than free text to the extent possible — the message set ensures proper closure of exchanges. Per 14-3-1(d-e).
HF, SATCOM, or Third Party. A voice backup must exist when CPDLC is being utilized. Per 14-3-1(c).

items.front: Oceanic CPDLC: transfer to CPDLC-capable facility
items.back: Automatic, accomplished within facility adapted parameters. Per 14-3-2(a).

items.front: Oceanic CPDLC: transfer to non-CPDLC-capable facility
items.back: Transfer of communications must be made in accordance with local directives and Letters of Agreement (LOAs). Per 14-3-2(b).

items.front: Oceanic CPDLC: failed automated transfer procedure
items.back: 1) Attempt manual transfer. 2) If unable, advise pilot to log on to next facility and send End Service (EOS) message. Per 14-3-3(a).

items.front: Oceanic CPDLC: connection lost on specific aircraft
items.back: Send connection request (CR1) OR advise pilot via backup communications to log on again. Per 14-3-3(c).
MUST advise pilot as early as possible for smooth transition. SHOULD (workload permitting) also advise reason for termination. Per 14-3-3(d).
Continue to protect airspace until operational response received. If no response, initiate action to ensure receipt. On initial voice contact: '(Call Sign) CPDLC Failure, (message).' Per 14-3-3(e).
